steps.title: Subtract 40 from both sides
steps.content:
Starting with $12x + 40 = 160$, subtract $40$ from both sides:
$$12x = 160 - 40 = 120$$

steps.title: Identify the equivalent equation
steps.content: The original equation is equivalent to $12x = 120$, which has the same solution ($x = 10$).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• A. $12x = 40$ gives $x = \frac{10}{3}$; different solution.
  • B. $12x = 80$ gives $x = \frac{20}{3}$; different solution.
  • C. $12x = 120$ gives $x = 10$; matches the original.
  • D. $12x = 160$ gives $x = \frac{40}{3}$; different solution.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
